name: Gmail
description: Read, label, and send mail from the connected Google account. One template, selectable permission levels: pick Read mail / Manage labels / Send mail at install (read-only by default), connect the mailbox on the agent's Deployment tab, and the agent gets gmail__* tools via eve's OpenAPI connection — access tokens self-refresh at runtime, and the granted level is visible to the agent so it never promises what it can't do.

scopeGroups.label: Read mail
scopeGroups.description: Search and read messages and attachments in the connected mailbox.

scopeGroups.label: Manage labels
scopeGroups.description: Apply and remove labels, mark mail read, archive — includes read access.

scopeGroups.label: Send mail
scopeGroups.description: Send messages as the connected account.

secrets.name: GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID
secrets.description: OAuth client id of Eden's shared Google app — provisioned automatically at deploy from the agent's Google connection. Used to refresh access tokens.

secrets.name: GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET
secrets.description: OAuth client secret of Eden's shared Google app — provisioned automatically at deploy. Needed for the refresh-token grant.

secrets.name: GOOGLE_OAUTH_REFRESH_TOKEN
secrets.description: The agent's Google refresh token, captured when you connect Google on the Deployment tab — provisioned automatically at deploy (sealed in Eden's database until then).

secrets.name: GOOGLE_OAUTH_SCOPES
secrets.description: The scopes the connected account actually granted, space-joined — provisioned automatically at deploy so the agent can read its own permission level.

setup:
**Pick permissions at install:** the install wizard shows three levels — **Read mail** (default), **Manage labels**, and **Send mail**. Tick what this agent needs; you can change the selection later on the agent's **Deployment** tab (adding a level asks for one reconnect).

**Connect the right mailbox:** after installing, open the agent's **Deployment** tab and click **Connect Google** — signed in as the mailbox you want the agent to act on. A shared mailbox like `billing@your-domain.com` just consents itself (sign in as it, or have its owner click Connect) — no domain-wide delegation involved.

**Self-host note (operators):** configure ONE Google OAuth client for this Eden installation before the Connect button works.

1. In **Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services**, enable the **Gmail API**.
2. Under **Credentials**, create an **OAuth client ID** of type **Web application** (or reuse the one from the Google Sheets connector — one client serves every Google connection).
3. Add `<origin>/google/callback` to its **Authorized redirect URIs** (e.g. `https://eden.example.com/google/callback`, and `http://localhost:5173/google/callback` for local dev).
4. Set `EDEN_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` and `EDEN_GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET` on the control plane.

**Publishing status matters** — the `gmail.*` scopes are **restricted**, Google's strictest tier:
- **Internal** (Google Workspace org only): no verification, refresh tokens do **not** expire. The right choice for a self-hosted Eden serving one organization.
- **External + Testing**: up to 100 test users, no verification, but refresh tokens **expire after 7 days**, so agents must be reconnected weekly. Fine for evaluation.
- **External + Production**: Google requires app verification for restricted scopes — a brand review **plus an annual third-party security assessment**. Plan for this only if you expose the connector to users outside your own Workspace.

Pick **Internal** if you self-host for one Workspace org.
